Based on the search results, here's information about Yash Highvoltage Limited:

## Company Profile

Yash Highvoltage Limited is an India-based company. Yash Highvoltage Limited was originally incorporated in the name of Yash Highvoltage Insulators Private Limited on June 06, 2002 at Gujarat. The company was converted into a Public Company on February 19, 2018 and the name was changed to Yash Highvoltage Limited on March 07, 2018 at Ahmedabad.

## Products & Business

The company is engaged in the manufacturing and distribution of a wide range of transformer bushings—Oil Impregnated Paper (OIP) condenser bushing, Resin Impregnated Paper (RIP) / Resin Impregnated Synthetic (RIS) condenser bushing, High Voltage and High Current bushing, OIP Wall bushing and Oil to Oil bushing. The company also undertakes repairing, retrofitting and replacement services of old bushings.

## Manufacturing Capacity

The company's plant is located in Dist. Savli, Vadodara, which has an installed capacity for production of 2500 units of OIP Bushings, 500 units of High Current Bushings, 1500 units of RIP Bushings and 1200 units of FRP cylinders & Guard Rings.

## Listing Status

Note: The company is not listed on NSE. The search results indicate it is listed on the BSE (Bombay Stock Exchange).

## Recent IPO

The company raised Rs 110.01 Crore through Initial Public Offer by issuing 75,35,000 equity shares in December, 2024.

Based on the search results, here are the key news items about Yash High Voltage Limited:

## Financial Results 2025

In H1 FY26, the company witnessed a 79% year-on-year increase in revenues, rising to ₹102 crore, along with a doubling of PAT. For FY26, Yash Highvoltage reports revenue of ₹2350 Mn with an EBITDA margin at 25.7%, and an order book stands at ₹400 Cr.

## Orders and Growth

These results have been driven by substantial orders from domestic utilities and expanding export business. In H2, revenue surged 45% year-on-year, reaching 1.35B rupees from 931M.

## Expansion Plans

A Vastu Pujan (groundbreaking ceremony) was held at the Savli expansion site, with commissioning expected by Q1 FY27 and trial production in Q2 FY27. The IPO proceeds are being channelled into a state-of-the-art RIP/RIS facility, expanding the product portfolio up to 550 kV and opening access to a broader addressable market.

## International Expansion

Yash Highvoltage has opened its sales office in the US and established agency and distribution agreement with Weidmann Electrical Technology AG for strengthening select European and North African markets footprint.

## Strategic Acquisition

Yash Highvoltage Limited completed the acquisition of 50% equity stake in Sukrut Electric Company Private Limited for ₹5.24 crores, creating a joint venture with Quality Power Electrical Equipments Limited.

# Yash Highvoltage Ltd - Financial Analysis

## Annual Financial Data (FY2020-FY2026, in Rs. Crores)

| Metric | FY20 | FY21 | FY22 | FY23 | FY24 | FY25 | FY26 |
|--------|------|------|------|------|------|------|------|
| Sales | 38 | 37 | 65 | 90 | 108 | 150 | 235 |
| Net Profit | 3 | 4 | 9 | 11 | 12 | 21 | 37 |
| OPM % | 16% | 20% | 22% | 21% | 20% | 21% | 24% |
| EPS (Rs) | 9.14 | 12.68 | 28.32 | 37.13 | 40.09 | 7.50 | 13.08 |

## Growth Metrics

- **5-Year Sales CAGR:** 44%
- **5-Year Profit CAGR:** 58% (noted as strong achievement)
- **3-Year Sales CAGR:** 38%
- **3-Year Profit CAGR:** 50%
- **TTM Sales Growth:** 57%

## Return Metrics

- **ROE (Last Year):** 23.5%
- **ROE (3-Year Average):** 24.4%
- **ROCE (5-Year):** 28.7%

## Balance Sheet Highlights (FY26)

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Total Assets | 264 Cr |
| Total Liabilities | 264 Cr |
| Borrowings | 38 Cr |
| Equity Capital | 14 Cr |
| Reserves | 170 Cr |
| Fixed Assets | 61 Cr |
| CWIP (Capital Work in Progress) | 43 Cr |

## Shareholding Pattern (Mar 2026)

- **Promoters:** 57.94%
- **DIIs:** 11.06%
- **Public:** 30.96%
- **FIIs:** 0.05%
- **Total Shareholders:** 3,741

## Key Ratios & Metrics

- **P/E Ratio:** 54.0
- **Price-to-Book:** 11.4x
- **Book Value:** 64.4 per share
- **Dividend Yield:** 0.14%
- **Market Cap:** 2,102 Cr
- **Current Share Price:** 735 (as of Jun 12)

## Half-Yearly Results (FY26)

| Period | Sales | Op Profit | Net Profit | EPS |
|--------|-------|-----------|-----------|-----|
| H1 FY26 | 100 | 21 | 14 | 4.91 |
| H2 FY26 | 136 | 36 | 24 | 8.31 |

## Working Capital Metrics (FY26)

- **Debtor Days:** 47
- **Inventory Days:** 194
- **Days Payable:** 60
- **Cash Conversion Cycle:** 182 days

## Flagged Concerns

The analysis identifies that "stock is trading at 11.4 times its book value," suggesting elevated valuation relative to tangible asset value.

## Recent Developments

- New manufacturing facility expansion at Vadodara underway
- Commissioning targeted for Q1 FY27; trial production expected Q2 FY27
- Unmodified auditor opinion on FY26 results

# Yash Highvoltage Analysis Summary

**Business Model**
Specialized manufacturer of high-voltage transformer bushings—critical insulation components in power transmission. The company operates on an "order-led rather than capacity-led" basis with "7,000 bushings per year" capacity, emphasizing disciplined growth over volume expansion.

**Customers & Concentration**
Heavily concentrated domestic focus: approximately "95 percent domestic and 4 percent exports" revenue in FY24. Top 10 customers account for "close to 80 percent of revenues." Primary clients are major Indian utilities (NTPC, PGCIL). High switching costs stem from long approval cycles and vendor certification requirements.

**Competitive Position**
First Indian company to localize RIP and RIS dry-type bushing technology. Offers "interchangeable solutions compatible with global OEMs up to 245kV and 25kA." Technical collaboration with Swiss firm MGC provides technology depth. Over "40,000 bushings installed globally."

**Industry Tailwinds**
- Aging global power infrastructure requiring replacement
- Renewable energy grid integration needs
- Rising transformer voltage and capacity requirements
- High barriers to entry due to safety-critical certification requirements
- Mission-critical positioning enables pricing power

**Industry Headwinds**
Dependence on power capex cycles; vulnerability to infrastructure spending delays.

**Raw Materials**
Not specifically discussed in the article.

**Risks Identified**
- Customer concentration (mitigated by sticky relationships)
- Power capex cycle dependency
- Technological obsolescence risk
- Overseas expansion execution risk
- Emerging competitive threat: "Transformer and rectifier are also coming up with their bushing plant" (reader comment)

**Exports**
Minimal current export penetration (~4% of revenue), but expansion underway through newly established "Yash Highvoltage USA Inc in 2025" for Americas market entry.

**Governance/Red Flags**
None explicitly mentioned. Company holds ISO 9001, ISO 14001 certifications and manufactures to "IEC and IEEE standards."

input.prompt: Summarize the Yash Highvoltage acquisition of 50% stake in Sukrut Electric Company: deal value, structure, rationale, the JV with Quality Power, and any dates. What does Sukrut do?

content.content:
# Yash Highvoltage's Sukrut Electric Acquisition

**Deal Value & Structure:**
Yash Highvoltage completed a ₹5.24 crore acquisition of exactly 50% equity stake in Sukrut Electric Company Private Limited. The transaction involved purchasing "20,42,754 equity shares of face value ₹100 each" and was finalized on January 9, 2026.

**Joint Venture:**
The acquisition created an equal partnership where Yash Highvoltage and Quality Power Electrical Equipments Limited each hold 50% ownership in Sukrut Electric, establishing a balanced joint venture structure.

**Strategic Rationale:**
Both companies operate in electrical equipment manufacturing, making this a strategic expansion rather than diversification. The deal aligns with Yash Highvoltage's core business operations and strengthens its position in the sector.

**Timeline:**
- Share Purchase Agreement signed: October 6, 2025
- Transaction completed: January 9, 2026

**Sukrut Electric's Business:**
Incorporated in December 1981 and based in Pune, Maharashtra, Sukrut Electric "specializes in manufacturing electronic equipment and instruments, panels, spares, accessories, and related fittings." The company reported ₹27.44 crore turnover in FY 2024-25 with ₹40.85 crore paid-up capital.

**Governance:**
Yash Highvoltage's Managing Director was nominated to Sukrut's Board following the acquisition.

Based on the search results, here's an overview of India's power transformer demand, transmission capex, and market outlook for 2025-2026:

## Market Size and Growth

The India Transformer market size is expected to grow from USD 3 billion in 2025 to USD 3.25 billion in 2026 and is forecast to reach USD 4.82 billion by 2031 at 8.22% CAGR over 2026-2031. Different sources provide varying projections, with the India Power Transformer Market valued at $3.8 Billion in 2026 expected to reach $8.05 Billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 8.70%.

## Transmission Capex Pipeline

India's power transmission and distribution (T&D) sector is set for a sustained growth trajectory, supported by a robust capital expenditure pipeline of about Rs. 9 trillion (US$ 96.70 billion) through 2032. Investments worth INR 17 lakh crore over 2016-2024, and a similar quantum under construction, are scaling transmission corridors.

## Key Growth Drivers

Rapid grid modernization, peak demand growth, and government funding collectively drive this growth path. Growth is underpinned by India's planned power transmission capacity expansion of over 50% by 2030, renewable energy targets, and industrial capex cycles. Additionally, peak demand has jumped from 130 GW in 2014 to 243 GW in 2024 and is projected to exceed 400 GW by 2030.

## Market Segments

Distribution units captured 58.92% of the India transformer market size in 2025 and are set to grow at an 8.84% CAGR during the outlook period. A power transformer comprises various windings, tap changing systems, a core, tank, and bushings.

## Yash Highvoltage IPO - Key Information

### IPO Details and Subscription

The Yash Highvoltage IPO is a book build issue of ₹110.01 crores, consisting of a fresh issue of 0.64 crore shares aggregating to ₹93.51 crores and offer for sale of 0.11 crore shares aggregating to ₹16.50 crores. The IPO bidding started from December 12, 2024 and ended on December 16, 2024, with allotment finalized on December 17, 2024, and shares got listed on BSE SME on December 19, 2024.

The subscription response was extraordinary: The IPO was subscribed 181.82 times on December 16, 2024, with the public issue subscribed 151.52 times in the retail category, 123.7 times in the QIB category, and 330.03 times in the NII category.

### Grey Market Premium (GMP)

The Yash Highvoltage IPO GMP made a high of ₹132 on December 11 against a low of ₹50 on December 10. The latest grey market premium stood at Rs 100 on December 16 morning, with shares expected to be listed at Rs 246 per equity share, indicating a gain percentage of 68.49 per cent.

### Customer Concentration and Key Clients

Yash has established itself as a trusted supplier to major Indian utilities including NTPC and PGCIL. However, while customer concentration exists, it is typical for this segment and partly offset by long approval cycles and sticky relationships.

### Risk Factors

Important risk considerations include general investment risks. IPO GMP is subject to extreme volatility, so an investment decision based solely on Yash Highvoltage SME IPO GMP will prove risky. Additionally, Yash Highvoltage Limited reported a Rs. 2.10 crore cyber fraud incident under SEBI regulations involving email impersonation by unidentified individuals posing as Chinese suppliers.

### Company Background

Incorporated in June 2002, Yash Highvoltage Limited specializes in the manufacturing and distributing a wide range of transformer bushings, including Oil-Impregnated Paper (OIP) condenser bushings, Resin-Impregnated Paper (RIP) and Resin-Impregnated Synthetic (RIS) condenser bushings, and also provides repair, retrofitting, and replacement services for old bushings.

input.prompt: Summarize this stock research on Yash Highvoltage: business, customers and concentration, competitors, margins, risks, raw materials, valuation concerns, and any red flags or governance issues mentioned.

content.content:
# Yash Highvoltage Cyber Fraud Incident Summary

**Amount & Breakdown:**
Total loss of Rs. 2.10 crores comprised two components: Rs. 1.36 crores for materials received on March 31, 2026, and Rs. 73.90 lacs for goods paid but never delivered.

**What Occurred:**
Fraudsters impersonated the company's regular Chinese supplier via email, requesting changes to bank account details. Despite the organization's request for proper documentation including exporter mandates and banker confirmations, the sophisticated transborder scheme succeeded in misdirecting payments to unidentified individuals.

**Disclosure Date:**
Reported to BSE Limited on March 31, 2026, under SEBI Regulation 30.

**Company Response:**
Yash Highvoltage initiated comprehensive remedial measures including "Internal investigation conducted by company team," formal regulatory complaints, evaluation with bankers for fund recovery, and strengthening of internal control mechanisms. The company emphasized that "the incident has no material impact on its operations."

**Governance Implications:**
The fraud involved no internal personnel, highlighting external cybersecurity vulnerabilities in international trade operations. The incident demonstrates the need for enhanced email verification protocols and supplier authentication procedures for transborder transactions.

REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Yash HighVoltage Stock Research Summary

## Business Model
Yash HighVoltage manufactures transformer bushings—safety components that allow high-voltage current to enter/exit transformers. The company specializes in condenser-graded bushings for voltage classes from 24kV to 245kV, serving approximately 60 countries through 30+ agents.

## Customers & Concentration
The analysis identifies 40,000+ products shipped globally but provides limited specifics on customer concentration. The research emphasizes that "7 bushings per power transformer" suggest significant OEM and utility relationships, though no major customer details are disclosed.

## Competitive Position
Yash competes in a highly concentrated market: only ~13 global manufacturers exist. The moat derives from "6–8 years for new entrants to build repeat trust," approval complexity, and supply constraints rather than manufacturing superiority.

## Margins & Financial Quality
Gross margins appear stable, with ROCE described as healthy. However, a critical weakness emerges: **CFO/PAT ratio weakened to 0.43 in FY25**, down from 1.17 in FY24. This cash-conversion deterioration is flagged as "the truth serum" and a major red flag.

## Key Risks & Red Flags

**Working Capital Crisis:**
The analyst identifies this as "the boss fight"—receivables and inventory growth threaten to consume cash despite profit growth. Debtor days trending above 90 or "spinning out of control" trigger exit recommendations.

**Execution Risks:**
- New plant delays or cost overruns
- Capex completion targets (Q3 CY26 prototypes, Q4 CY26 commercialization) remain unproven
- Export receivables quality unverified

**Governance Concerns:**
- Limited disclosure on receivables ageing and export credit terms
- Lack of transparency on approvals pipeline
- Related-party dealings not thoroughly examined

## Valuation Concerns
The research suggests "violent rerating" is possible only if cash conversion improves and plant execution succeeds. No explicit valuation target provided; upside assumes "premium multiples" contingent on governance and cash metrics.

## Raw Materials & Supply Chain
No specific raw material risks discussed; however, localization initiatives suggest current import dependence on certain components.

## Critical Governance Issues
The disclosure warns of potential "unpleasant governance surprises" and emphasizes monitoring for quality incidents, warranty provisions, and leverage spikes. The analysis remains cautious about transparency relative to growth claims.

content.text: I have comprehensive, cross-checked material. Let me get the company's own demand-outlook interview and a recent expansion update to round out the industry view and recent timeline.

input.prompt: Summarize what Yash Highvoltage management says about demand for RIP transformer bushings, market outlook, export plans, capacity expansion, and any specific drivers or challenges mentioned.

Summarize the latest Yash Highvoltage expansion update: the Savli/Vadodara facility, capacity, voltage class (550kV), RIP/RIS, timeline for commissioning and trial production, capex amount, and order book figures.

content.content:
# Yash Highvoltage: RIP Bushings Market Outlook

## Demand & Market Conditions

Management reports strong demand driven by data center expansion, urbanization, EV infrastructure, railway electrification, and renewable energy projects. According to Keyur Shah, "Demand is strong with rising global energy requirement due to rapidly rising data centre installations, urbanisation and" other factors pressuring power grids.

## Capacity Expansion Plans

The company's existing Halol facility produces approximately 3,000 RIP units annually. A new greenfield plant in Gujarat's Savli area is under advanced construction to "materially increase RIP capacity and reduce lead time, moving us from being a domestic supplier to a significantly larger one for both domestic and export markets."

## Export Strategy & Market Share

Management aims to strengthen market position through the new facility, upgraded testing capabilities, R&D investments, and the Sukrut Electric acquisition partnership. These initiatives target both domestic and export customer bases.

## Key Drivers & Challenges

**Drivers:** Grid modernization, renewable integration, government "Make-in-India" policies, and active utility procurement tenders (including POWERGRID).

**Challenges:** Import dependency on RIP cores and insulators remains, though the company is developing in-house production capabilities to reduce reliance on foreign materials.

# Yash Highvoltage Expansion Update

**Facility Location & Status**
The company is advancing its greenfield manufacturing facility in Vadodara at "Plot No. 32-A, Suncity Industrial Park, Village Haripura, Taluka Savli, District Vadodara." The office building construction has been completed, with equipment procurement finished and installation currently underway.

**Production Capacity & Technology**
The facility will manufacture Resin Impregnated Paper (RIP) and Resin Impregnated Synthetic (RIS) transformer bushings, with capability extended to 550 kV voltage class—representing an expansion from the company's previous 220 kV design capacity.

**Timeline**
- **Commissioning target:** End of Q1 FY 2026-27
- **Trial production start:** Q2 FY 2026-27
- **Commercial production:** H2 FY27

**Capital Investment**
The company has invested ₹5,920.15 Lakhs in property, plant, and equipment during the fiscal year, with expansion funded through IPO proceeds.

**Order Book**
The company maintains "an order book exceeding ₹400 crores, providing robust execution visibility for the next 12 to 24 months."

This expansion positions Yash Highvoltage to reduce import dependency for specialized high-voltage electrical components while supporting India's electrical infrastructure growth.

content.text:
I now have a thorough, cross-checked picture. Here is my synthesis.

---

# Yash Highvoltage Limited — Research Synthesis

**What the company is:** Yash Highvoltage Ltd (BSE SME: YASHHV / 544310; ISIN INE00GK01023) is a Vadodara (Savli), Gujarat-based manufacturer of high-voltage transformer bushings — OIP (oil-impregnated paper) and RIP/RIS (resin-impregnated paper/synthetic) condenser bushings, high-current bushings, wall bushings — plus repair/retrofit services. It is the first Indian company to localize dry-type RIP/RIS bushing technology and supplies major Indian utilities (NTPC, POWERGRID) and global OEMs, with interchangeable solutions up to 245 kV / 25 kA. It listed on the BSE SME platform on 19 Dec 2024. **Note:** It trades on BSE SME, *not* NSE, despite some pages labeling it "NSE/BSE."

*Caveat on financials:* India's fiscal year ends 31 March. Several aggregator pages confusingly label the year ending March 2026 as both "FY25" and "FY26." I use FY26 = year ended March 2026 below. Figures come largely from Screener and corporate-disclosure aggregators; I could not independently verify every line against the audited annual report.

---

## 1. Recent news & developments (last ~18 months)

- **IPO (Dec 2024):** Raised ₹110.01 cr (fresh issue ₹93.5 cr + OFS ₹16.5 cr) at ₹146/share. Subscribed ~182x overall (QIB ~124x, NII ~330x, retail ~152x); strong grey-market premium (~₹100–132). Proceeds earmarked for the new RIP/RIS facility and working capital. ([Chittorgarh](https://www.chittorgarh.com/ipo/yash-highvoltage-ipo/1929/), [IPO Watch](https://ipowatch.in/yash-highvoltage-ipo-subscription-status/))
- **FY26 results (approved ~13 May 2026):** Revenue ~₹235 cr (up ~57% YoY from ~₹150 cr); net profit ~₹37 cr (up from ~₹21 cr); OPM ~24–26%. H2 was much stronger than H1 (H2 sales ~₹136 cr, PAT ~₹24 cr vs H1 ~₹100 cr / ~₹14 cr). Auditor opinion reported as unmodified. ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **Order book ~₹400 cr**, described as 12–24 months of execution visibility. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))
- **Greenfield expansion (Savli/Vadodara):** New RIP/RIS plant extending capability to 550 kV (up from ~220–245 kV). Equipment installation underway; commissioning targeted end-Q1 FY27, trial production Q2 FY27, commercial production H2 FY27. ~₹59 cr capex in FY26. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144))
- **JV / acquisition:** Bought 50% of Sukrut Electric Company Pvt Ltd (Pune; electrical equipment/panels, FY25 turnover ~₹27 cr) for ₹5.24 cr, completed 9 Jan 2026, forming a 50:50 JV with Quality Power Electrical Equipments Ltd. MD nominated to Sukrut's board. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926))
- **International push:** Set up Yash Highvoltage USA Inc (2025); agency/distribution agreement with Weidmann Electrical Technology AG for parts of Europe/North Africa. ([tradebrains via search](https://tradebrains.in/yash-highvoltages-post-listing-surge-signals-a-high-voltage-growth-story/))
- **Planned fundraise:** Board considered raising up to ₹150 cr (for growth + debt repayment) around the 13 May 2026 meeting. Structure (QIP/preferential) and final approval not fully confirmed in public sources — treat as in-progress. ([search results](https://groww.in/stocks/yash-highvoltage-ltd))

---

## 2. Industry tailwinds & headwinds

**Tailwinds (well-corroborated across independent sources):**
- India T&D capex pipeline of ~₹9 trillion (~US$96.7 bn) through 2032; transmission capacity planned to expand >50% by 2030. ([IBEF](https://www.ibef.org/news/india-s-power-transmission-sector-to-see-us-96-70-billion-capex-push-by-2032))
- India transformer market ~US$3–3.8 bn (2025/26), growing ~8–8.7% CAGR to ~US$4.8–8 bn by early 2030s. ([Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market), [IMARC](https://www.imarcgroup.com/india-power-transformer-market))
- Peak demand rose 130 GW (2014) → 243 GW (2024), projected >400 GW by 2030 — structural grid build-out. ([Mordor](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market))
- Bushing-specific demand drivers cited by management and analysts: renewable grid integration, data centres, EV/railway electrification, ageing-infrastructure replacement, and a global shift toward dry-type RIP bushings. ([T&D India interview](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- High barriers to entry: long utility approval/certification cycles (~6–8 years to build repeat trust), only ~13 global bushing makers — supports pricing power and stickiness. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage), [deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))

**Headwinds:**
- Demand is tied to the power capex cycle; tender/infrastructure delays directly hit a small order-led supplier.
- Import dependency for RIP cores and insulators — supply-chain and FX exposure until in-house production matures (management's stated goal). ([T&D India](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/))
- Emerging domestic competition: at least one reader/analyst flags Transformers & Rectifiers (India) reportedly setting up its own bushing plant — a potential captive-supply threat. ([deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))

---

## 3. Company-specific risks

- **Customer concentration:** Top 10 customers ≈ 80% of revenue; heavy reliance on a few large utilities/OEMs (NTPC, POWERGRID). Partly mitigated by high switching costs/approvals. ([deepdivecaps](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through))
- **Working-capital intensity / cash conversion — the most concrete red flag:** Independent analysis flags CFO/PAT falling to ~0.43 in FY25 from ~1.17 in FY24, i.e., profits not converting to cash. Inventory days ~194 and cash-conversion cycle ~182 days (FY26) confirm a capital-heavy model where receivables/inventory growth can outrun profit. Debtor days (~47) look reasonable, but inventory is the pressure point. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage), [Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **Export receivables / new-market execution:** US and Europe/N. Africa expansion is early-stage; export credit terms and receivable quality are not transparently disclosed. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage))
- **Execution risk on the 550 kV plant:** Commissioning/commercialization timelines (commercial output only H2 FY27) are unproven; cost overruns or delays would dent the growth thesis.
- **Raw material / import & FX:** RIP cores and insulators imported (incl. from China) until localized.
- **Liquidity / SME-stock risk:** BSE SME listing means thinner liquidity, wider spreads, and lower disclosure granularity than mainboard names. Valuation is rich — ~P/E 54, ~11x book (as of mid-June 2026), leaving little margin for execution slippage. ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))

---

## 4. Red flags & governance concerns

- **Cyber/payment fraud (₹2.10 cr), disclosed 31 Mar 2026:** Fraudsters impersonated a Chinese supplier via email and redirected payments (₹1.36 cr for goods received + ₹73.9 lakh paid-but-undelivered). Company called it immaterial and says it has tightened controls and filed complaints. Financially small, but it points to **weak vendor-payment verification controls** — a governance/internal-controls signal worth monitoring, especially given import dependence. ([scanx](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-limited-reports-rs-2-10-crore-cyber-fraud-incident-under-sebi-regulations/36546063))
- **Disclosure gaps:** Independent analysts note limited transparency on receivables ageing, export credit terms, the approvals/order pipeline, and related-party dealings — typical of SME-stage reporting but relevant given the cash-conversion concern. ([anigup](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage))
- **Promoter holding ~58%** (Mar 2026); DII ~11%, FII ~0.05%, public ~31%. No pledging surfaced in the sources I reviewed (not explicitly confirmed). ([Screener](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/))
- **No outright fraud/SEBI-action/auditor-qualification** found against the company itself; FY26 auditor opinion reported as unmodified.

---

## What I could not fully verify / confidence notes

- I relied on aggregators (Screener, scanx, anigup, tradebrains, T&D India) rather than the audited annual report or BSE filings directly — exact financial line items, the fundraise's final structure, pledging status, and segment/customer disclosures should be confirmed against the **company's filings** (yashhv.com investor portal and BSE announcements).
- The TradeBrains source returned a 403 and could not be read in full; its details were captured only via search snippets.
- The CFO/PAT and competitive-threat (Transformers & Rectifiers) claims come from single analyst write-ups — directionally consistent with the high inventory days in Screener, but treat the specific ratio and competitor plan as not independently corroborated.
- Some pages mislabel the listing venue (BSE SME, not NSE) and mix up FY25/FY26 labeling.

**This is a findings report only — no buy/sell view is implied.**

Sources:
- [Screener — Yash Highvoltage financials](https://www.screener.in/company/544310/)
- [DeepDiveCaps — sector/business deep dive](https://deepdivecaps.substack.com/p/powering-grid-reliability-through)
- [Anigup — stock research / red flags](https://www.anigup.com/post/yash-highvoltage)
- [scanx — Sukrut Electric acquisition](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/orders-deals/yash-highvoltage-limited-completes-5-24-crore-acquisition-of-50-stake-in-sukrut-electric-company/29589926)
- [scanx — expansion / Q2 FY27 trial production](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-advances-expansion-targets-trial-production-in-q2-fy-2026-27/41325144)
- [scanx — ₹2.10 cr cyber fraud disclosure](https://scanx.trade/stock-market-news/companies/yash-highvoltage-limited-reports-rs-2-10-crore-cyber-fraud-incident-under-sebi-regulations/36546063)
- [T&D India — management interview on RIP demand](https://www.tndindia.com/we-see-strong-demand-for-rip-transformer-bushings-yash-highvoltage/)
- [Chittorgarh — IPO details](https://www.chittorgarh.com/ipo/yash-highvoltage-ipo/1929/)
- [IPO Watch — IPO subscription](https://ipowatch.in/yash-highvoltage-ipo-subscription-status/)
- [IBEF — India T&D capex pipeline](https://www.ibef.org/news/india-s-power-transmission-sector-to-see-us-96-70-billion-capex-push-by-2032)
- [Mordor Intelligence — India transformer market](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/india-transformer-market)
- [IMARC — India power transformer market](https://www.imarcgroup.com/india-power-transformer-market)
- [Groww — stock page / fundraise mention](https://groww.in/stocks/yash-highvoltage-ltd)
